2009-07-01.title: Iraqis Celebrate U.S. Troop Withdrawals
2009-07-01.abstract: Iraqis celebrated the withdrawal of American combat forces with parades and a national holiday.

2009-07-01.title: Obama’s Stance Deflects Chávez’s Finger-Pointing
2009-07-01.abstract: President Hugo Chávez’s insinuations of U.S. involvement in Honduras’s coup are being deflected by the U.S. response.

2009-07-01.title: Argentina: Swine Flu’s Spread Leads to School Closings
2009-07-01.abstract: Authorities in Buenos Aires announced that they were canceling classes for thousands of students to try to contain a fast-spreading outbreak of swine flu.

2009-07-01.title: Zimbabwe Says China Is Giving It Loans
2009-07-01.abstract: Zimbabwe’s prime minister, Morgan Tsvangirai, said an appointee had secured $950 million in credit from China, a longtime ally of Robert Mugabe.

2009-07-01.title: Compromise Is Sought to Honduras Standoff
2009-07-01.abstract: A flurry of negotiations began on Wednesday to lay the groundwork for a possible return of the nation’s ousted president, Manuel Zelaya.

2009-07-02.title: Insurgents Hail Pullout of Troops From Cities
2009-07-02.abstract: Groups that have advocated violence against Americans urged Iraqis to continue the fight, but not among themselves.

2009-07-02.title: Mexico: Migrants’ Money Sent From U.S. Fell in May
2009-07-02.abstract: The decrease — comparing the same month in two consecutive years — is the largest since Mexico’s central bank began tracking remittances in 1995.

2009-07-02.title: Zimbabwe: Trial Ordered for Senior Party Official
2009-07-02.abstract: Roy Bennett of the Movement for Democratic Change, will be tried Oct. 13 on terrorism charges his party says were trumped up, his lawyer, Trust Maanda, said.

2009-07-02.title: Greece: Another Attempt to Curb Smoking
2009-07-02.abstract: Greece adopted smoking restrictions Wednesday that said workplaces and many public places must be smoke-free.

2009-07-02.title: Israel: Court Orders Stiffer Charges in Shooting
2009-07-02.abstract: A commander and a soldier involved in shooting a rubber bullet in the foot of a bound and blindfolded Palestinian had been charged with “unbecoming conduct,” which does not carry a criminal record.

2009-07-03.title: Mexican Official Seeks Arrest of Day Care Center’s Owners
2009-07-03.abstract: A month after a fire in a day-care center killed 48 children, Mexico’s attorney general is seeking the arrest of the nursery’s politically connected owners.

2009-07-03.title: U.S. to Donate Drug to Combat Swine Flu
2009-07-03.abstract: The United States will donate 420,000 packets of the antiviral drug Tamiflu to the Pan-American Health Organization to help fight the swine flu pandemic in Latin America and the Caribbean.

2009-07-03.title: Activists Held by Israel for Trying to Break Gaza Blockade
2009-07-03.abstract: Nineteen foreign activists of the pro-Palestinian Free Gaza Movement were being held in Israel awaiting deportation on Thursday, two days after the Israeli Navy seized control of their boat off Gaza.

2009-07-03.title: Envoy Prepares to Visit Honduras, Warning of Obstacles
2009-07-03.abstract: The head of the Organization of American States said he was prepared to call for sanctions if he failed during a visit to the country.

2009-07-03.title: Britain Asks Allies for Help on Employees Held in Iran
2009-07-03.abstract: Britain continued to push other European countries on Thursday to take a tough stance against Iran for detaining at least one employee of the British Embassy in Tehran.

2009-07-04.title: Top Reformers Admitted Plot, Iran Declares
2009-07-04.abstract: The Iranian government has made it a practice to publicize confessions from political prisoners, often subject to sleep deprivation, solitary confinement and torture, rights groups say.

2009-07-04.title: Swine Flu Death Toll in Argentina Climbs
2009-07-04.abstract: Argentina’s president said she would not rule out closing major public venues where swine flu could spread more quickly.

2009-07-04.title: Italy: Toll in Train Accident Reaches 22
2009-07-04.abstract: The death toll from a train accident this week rose Friday to 22 after three hospitalized people died.

2009-07-04.title: France: Singer Sentenced to Prison
2009-07-04.abstract: A French court sentenced the popular Algerian singer known as Cheb Mami to five years in prison for abducting a former lover in 2005 and forcing her to undergo an abortion.

2009-07-04.title: Venezuela: Radio Stations Threatened
2009-07-04.abstract: Diosdado Cabello, a top aide to President Hugo Chávez, said the government would revoke the licenses of 240 radio stations across the country.

2009-07-05.title: Clerical Leaders Defy Ayatollah on Iran Election
2009-07-05.abstract: An important group of religious leaders in Iran called for the results to be thrown out, the most public sign of a major split in the clerical establishment.

2009-07-05.title: Black Boxes Are Detected From Yemeni Jet
2009-07-05.abstract: France announced on Sunday that search teams had detected the acoustic beacons from the two flight recorders of a Yemeni jet that crashed last week in the Indian Ocean.

2009-07-05.title: Campaign Quiets Down in Indonesia Ahead of Elections
2009-07-05.abstract: Indonesia will choose its president on Wednesday, after three weeks of campaigning in which five men and one woman crisscrossed the country to get their message out.

2009-07-05.title: Obama Raises Concerns About Freedom and Judicial Independence in Russia
2009-07-05.abstract: President Obama raised concerns about the treatment of a businessman, Mikhail B. Khodorkovsky, who along with his partner has been put back on trial six years after they were first arrested.

2009-07-05.title: Riots in Western China Amid Ethnic Tension
2009-07-05.abstract: At least 1,000 rioters clashed with the police as disputes between Muslim Uighurs and Han Chinese grew.

2009-07-06.title: Center-Right Urges Support of the Left in a French Election
2009-07-06.abstract: The far-right National Front lost a local council election after the country’s governing Socialist Party urged voters to support the left.

2009-07-06.title: As Obama Visits, Russian TV Alters Take on U.S.
2009-07-06.abstract: In sync with the Kremlin, the Russian media have muted their criticism of the United States.

2009-07-06.title: With No Clear Path Out of a Diplomatic Thicket, a Push to Redraw the Map
2009-07-06.abstract: Rather than tying negotiations to the treatment of Daw Aung San Suu Kyi, say experts, the world should engage the junta on a broad range of issues.

2009-07-06.title: America Searches for Means of Influence in Iraq
2009-07-06.abstract: In dealing with Iraqi politics, American diplomats — who have a reputation for being heavy-handed — must find a new tone.

2009-07-06.title: European Union Official to Let Term Expire
2009-07-06.abstract: Javier Solana, the union’s foreign policy chief, was widely expected to step down, though he had not before been so explicit in public about his future.

2009-07-07.title: Venezuela: Caracas Mayor on Hunger Strike
Germany: New Bravery Medals
2009-07-07.abstract: Four German soldiers who served in Afghanistan were awarded the first medals for bravery the country has given since World War II.

2009-07-07.title: With Wounded Pride, Unemployed Koreans Quietly Turn to Manual Labor
2009-07-07.abstract: Former white-collar workers go to great lengths to keep their new jobs, like deckhand on a crab boat or back-scrubber in a bathhouse, from family and friends.

2009-07-07.title: In Latest Upheaval, China Applies New Strategies to Control Flow of Information
2009-07-07.abstract: In the wake of deadly riots, China is deploying new methods not just to suppress bad news at the source, but to spin whatever unflattering tidbits escape its control.

2009-07-07.title: As Premier Faces Scandal, Italy Sets Stage for G-8
2009-07-07.abstract: The summit meeting set to open Wednesday is set in an earthquake zone beset by tremors and is being organized by a prime minister fending off a sex scandal.

2009-07-08.title: Britain: Police Criticized for Handling of Protesters
2009-07-08.abstract: The police were too intent on preventing violence and did not adequately plan for the thousands of peaceful protesters, an independent agency said in a report.

2009-07-08.title: Team Monitoring Diamond Trade Rebukes Zimbabwe
2009-07-08.abstract: The findings by the team, whose goal is to halt trade in so-called blood diamonds, could complicate the country’s ability to sell its diamonds on international markets.

2009-07-08.title: In Iran, a Struggle Beyond the Streets
2009-07-08.abstract: Among Iran’s elite, there is unease that the system they rely on for power has been stripped of its legitimacy.

2009-07-08.title: Tracking Faraway Action From an Iraqi Base
2009-07-08.abstract: As American soldiers in Iraq withdraw into their bases, their comrades in Afghanistan are running more missions.

2009-07-08.title: In Russia, Obama’s Star Power Does Not Translate
2009-07-08.abstract: Unlike other capitals, Moscow has greeted President Obama as just another dignitary passing through.

2009-07-09.title: Polishing a Lost Gem to Dazzle Tourists
2009-07-09.abstract: Officials in a poor town hope that a 1,700-year-old mosaic floor, one of the largest and finest tiled floors in Israel, will help restore their city’s past glory.

2009-07-09.title: Nebulous Sunni Insurgent Urges Attacks
2009-07-09.abstract: The Iraqi government had said the insurgent leader had been captured earlier this year.

2009-07-09.title: Iran Releases Protesters, but Still Holds 500
2009-07-09.abstract: A human rights group in New York says the government is continuing to make arrests after demonstrations protesting the disputed presidential election.

2009-07-09.title: Venezuela Halts Oil Flow to Honduras
2009-07-09.abstract: The Venezuelan oil minister, Rafael Ramírez, said shipments of oil would not resume until that country’s ousted president, Manuel Zelaya, was reinstated.

2009-07-09.title: Migrants Describe Grief From China’s Strife
2009-07-09.abstract: The journey of a family trying to forge a new life in the Xinjiang region ended with the death of a son in ethnic riots.

2009-07-10.title: Brussels: Bloc Backs Barroso Term
2009-07-10.abstract: European Union leaders formally endorsed José Manuel Barroso, left, the European Commission president, for a second five-year term.

2009-07-10.title: Turkey: Visa for a Uighur Leader
2009-07-10.abstract: Prime Minister Recep Tayyip Erdogan said that Turkey would grant a visa to Rebiya Kadeer, a Uighur political activist, if she wanted to travel to Turkey.

2009-07-10.title: The Buses of Bogotá
2009-07-10.abstract: Over the last decade, cities in developing countries around the world have been adopting bus systems like Bogotá's Transmilenio, which eases traffic and pollution.

2009-07-10.title: China’s President Vows to ‘Deal a Blow’ to Protesters
2009-07-10.abstract: President Hu Jintao called for stability and promised harsh punishment for those found to have played a role in the disturbances that have killed scores of people and injured more than a thousand.

2009-07-10.title: Kurds Defy Baghdad, Laying Claim to Land and Oil
2009-07-10.abstract: With little notice, Kurdistan is moving forward on a new constitution, alarming some Iraqis and Americans.

2009-07-11.title: Spain: Bull Kills Man at Pamplona
2009-07-11.abstract: A Spanish man was fatally gored by a bull at the San Fermín festival in Pamplona on Friday, the first such fatality in 14 years at the event.

2009-07-11.title: South Africa: World Cup Projects Could Resume After Labor Vote
2009-07-11.abstract: Striking construction workers who laid down their tools on Wednesday will vote on a revised offer from their union negotiators and a consortium of employers.

2009-07-11.title: South Korea: Computer Virus Is Traced to Sites in 5 Nations
2009-07-11.abstract: The virus behind a wave of cyberattacks that hobbled government Web sites in South Korea and the United States was distributed by host Web sites based in five countries, the Korea Communications Commission said.

2009-07-11.title: A Strongman Is China’s Rock in Ethnic Strife
2009-07-11.abstract: Wang Lequan, who has used an iron hand against Uighur Muslims, is Beijing’s go-to expert on policies toward ethnic minorities.

2009-07-11.title: Ghana Visit Highlights Scarce Stability in Africa
2009-07-11.abstract: Ghana’s democratic elections and comparatively well-managed economy are still not the norm in Africa, and the list of imploding, unstable countries is long.

2009-07-12.title: 6 Are Killed and 67 Hurt in Bombings in Iraq Cities
2009-07-12.abstract: The attack in Mosul killed at least 4 and wounded 35 and involved a car bomb in the neighborhood of Kokjali, which is mainly inhabited by the Shabak ethnic minority.

2009-07-12.title: The Sutil/Raikkonen Story
2009-07-12.abstract: No story at the German Grand Prix can quite top that of Mark Webber winning the race in his 130th race in the series and after a hellish winter with a broken leg. But certainly the weirdest – and saddest – story in the race was that of Adrian Sutil and Kimi Raikkonen colliding.

2009-07-12.title: Roadside Bomb Kills 2 Marines in Southern Afghanistan
2009-07-12.abstract: Two United States Marines were killed in a roadside explosion in the southern province of Helmand, a United States forces spokeswoman said Sunday.

2009-07-12.title: Churches and Envoy Attacked in Iraq
2009-07-12.abstract: At least four people were killed in blasts at five Christian churches, and the American ambassador to Iraq narrowly escaped injury in another attack.

2009-07-12.title: Collapse of a Subway Track in India Kills 5 and Injures 13
2009-07-12.abstract: The accident on Sunday morning is the second deadly incident in the past nine months on New Delhi’s subway system, known as the Metro.

2009-07-13.title: Barren Berry Season Leads to Far Richer Discovery
2009-07-13.abstract: Two Swedish grandmothers, berry pickers and amateur geologists, found what may be the largest gold deposits in Europe right outside their tiny town.

2009-07-13.title: N. Korean Leader Dying of Cancer, Report Says
2009-07-13.abstract: Kim Jong-il has pancreatic cancer and the illness is life-threatening, the South Korean broadcaster YTN said.

2009-07-13.title: War Crimes Trial to Hear From Ex-Liberia President
2009-07-13.abstract: Charles Taylor will take the stand this week in a trial that has heard accounts of rape and other atrocities during the war in the 1990s in Sierra Leone.

2009-07-13.title: Mexico Posts Are Blitzed After Arrest in Drug War
2009-07-13.abstract: It took six months of intelligence work for the police to corner a man suspected of being one of western Mexico’s top drug bosses. But retaliation came swiftly.

2009-07-13.title: Fuse of Fear, Lit in China, Has Victims on 2 Sides
2009-07-13.abstract: Two narratives of victimhood have emerged from the violence between Uighurs and Han Chinese.

2009-07-14.title: Niger Senses a Threat to Its Scrap of Democracy
2009-07-14.abstract: Tens of thousands have taken to the streets to protest President Mamadou Tandja’s plan to stay beyond the legal limit of two terms.

2009-07-14.title: As Mumbai Spills Over, Floodwater Creeps Closer
2009-07-14.abstract: The disappearance of Mumbai’s wetlands, along with the degradation of its waterways, have made it more vulnerable to flooding.

2009-07-14.title: Zimbabwe: Mugabe Backers Disrupt Conference
2009-07-14.abstract: Supporters of President Robert Mugabe disrupted the opening day of a national conference to draw up a new constitution.

2009-07-14.title: Indonesia: Missing Officer Found Dead Near Mine
2009-07-14.abstract: A police officer who was reported missing on Sunday was found dead on Monday near a mine operated in Papua by Freeport, the American conglomerate.

2009-07-15.title: Cautious Move to Free Detained Executive in China
2009-07-15.abstract: Australia’s Prime Minister Kevin Rudd is wary of taking a harsh stand with Beijing in his efforts to gain the release of a Rio Tinto mining official.

2009-07-15.abstract: A legal battle over the relationship between a deceased eccentric billionaire and a younger married man has enraptured the public.

2009-07-15.title: Mining Company Inquiry Puts Focus on a Black Market in China’s Steel Industry
2009-07-15.abstract: People working in the industry have long complained about bribery, deceit and a system turned rotten.

2009-07-15.title: With Help, Conductor and Wife Ended Lives
2009-07-15.abstract: Even among supporters of decriminalizing assisted suicide, the death of Sir Edward Downes and his wife raised troubling questions.

2009-07-15.title: Rwanda: Ex-Governor Convicted for Role in Rwandan Genocide
2009-07-15.abstract: A United Nations tribunal convicted the former governor of Rwanda’s capital, Kigali, on Tuesday for his role in the Rwandan genocide.

2009-07-16.title: Kenya’s Bill for Bloodshed Nears Payment
2009-07-16.abstract: Since last year’s post-election violence, Kenyans have waited to hear who masterminded the carnage.

2009-07-16.title: Russian Weapon Is in Need of Rescue
2009-07-16.abstract: A missile to propel the Russian military into the 21st century is having trouble propelling itself.

2009-07-16.title: Warning From General on End to Afghan Combat

2009-07-16.title: For Clinton, ’09 Campaign Is for Her Turf
2009-07-16.abstract: In a major speech, the secretary of state seemed to battle a perception that the State Department has lost influence to an assertive White House.

2009-07-16.title: Israel Sees Evidence of Hezbollah’s Rearming in Explosion
2009-07-16.abstract: Israel said a Hezbollah arms cache in a building that exploded in southern Lebanon violated the United Nations resolution that brought an end to the 2006 Israel-Hezbollah war.

2009-07-17.abstract: Epidemiologists have pointed out that, in reality, millions have had swine flu, usually in a mild form, so numbers of laboratory-confirmed cases were actually meaningless.

2009-07-17.abstract: Adm. Mike Mullen said Pakistan’s army was defeating Taliban militants in the Swat Valley and dealing effectively with displaced people.

2009-07-17.abstract: Two radical Islamist groups made the agreement after a renegade militia marched two Frenchmen out of their hotel on Tuesday.

2009-07-18.abstract: The Times's C.J. Chivers describes how Natalya Estemirova, one of the premier human rights investigators in the Caucasus, helped him with an article.

2009-07-18.abstract: Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton arrived in India amid fears that the United States was too preoccupied with events in Afghanistan and Pakistan.

2009-07-18.abstract: An investigation that former President Néstor Kirchner called a national disgrace has received new urgency and is raising hopes among Jewish groups.

2009-07-18.abstract: The state-run human rights agency named more than 200 suspects as the perpetrators of the post-election violence that killed more than 1,000 people last year.

2009-07-19.abstract: Authorities are painstakingly looking to obtain more information about the two bombers who set off blasts at two hotels on Sunday.

2009-07-19.abstract: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu refused to stop Jewish building in East Jerusalem, saying Israel’s sovereignty over the city could not be challenged.

2009-07-19.abstract: India used a visit by Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton to dig in its heels against legally binding targets to reduce emissions of carbon dioxide.

2009-07-19.abstract: The four employees of the mining giant accused of staling state documents could be spared the more serious spying charges, the Australian foreign minister said.

2009-07-20.abstract: Since ethnic clashes erupted two weeks ago in Urumqi, security forces have detained hundreds of Uighurs blamed for the slaughter.

2009-07-20.abstract: In the weeks since Benjamin Netanyahu accepted the principle of a Palestinian state, officials are questioning his commitment to a final peace accord.

2009-07-20.abstract: A plan to overhaul the troubled U.S.-run prison at Bagram Air Base and other facilities was prompted by worries about militant recruiting in the prisons.

2009-07-20.abstract: The military provided few details about the death, saying only in a statement that it was a “combat-related incident as a result of enemy action” in Anbar Province.

2009-07-20.abstract: Mohammad Khatami made a bold challenge to the government by questioning its legitimacy after another former president said the state had lost Iranians’ trust.

2009-07-21.abstract: Defense Secretary Robert M. Gates announced a temporary increase in troops in Iraq and Afghanistan.

2009-07-21.abstract: Mr. Kolakowski had embraced, and then rejected, the intellectual pillars of Communism and had been credited with serving a crucial role in Poland’s fate.

2009-07-21.title: Peru: Former President Sentenced
2009-07-21.abstract: Alberto Fujimori was sentenced to seven and a half years in prison for paying a $15 million bribe to his former spymaster, Vladimiro Montesinos, in 2000.

2009-07-21.title: Georgian Leader Unveils Reforms
2009-07-21.abstract: President Mikheil Saakashvili is hoping to quiet long-simmering complaints about his leadership before a visit by Vice President Joseph R. Biden Jr.

2009-07-21.title: State Ruled by Crime and Chávez Family
2009-07-21.abstract: Barinas is the setting for a terrifying surge in abductions, making it a contender for Latin America’s most likely place to get kidnapped.

2009-07-22.title: Express Route to Divorce Has Familiar Delays
2009-07-22.abstract: A new no-fault divorce law removes some obstacles, but doesn’t necessarily speed up the process.

2009-07-22.title: All Eyepieces on Jupiter After a Big Impact
2009-07-22.abstract: An amateur astronomer in Australia took one last look through his telescope and discovered the site of a new comet strike on Jupiter.

2009-07-22.abstract: The official, Esfandiar Rahim Mashaei, had angered conservatives with his remarks on Israel.

2009-07-22.abstract: Pakistani officials have told the U.S. that the Marines fighting the Taliban in southern Afghanistan will force militants across the border into Pakistan.

2009-07-22.abstract: India’s top airline security agency filed a complaint against Continental Airlines after the carrier subjected a former president of the country to a routine security check.

2009-07-23.abstract: Kashgar has been besieged by fear since ethnic rioting two weeks ago claimed at least 197 lives in Urumqi in western China.

2009-07-23.abstract: President Obama praised the country’s leader after he reversed a decision to close an American air base.

2009-07-23.abstract: Beijing responded to accusations that the government line on minorities contributed to recent violence in Xinjiang and the riots in Tibet last year.

2009-07-23.abstract: The United Nations has already endorsed a “responsibility to protect” people from atrocities, but the validity of the policy may face another debate.

2009-07-23.abstract: After their first meeting since the United States handed over military control to Iraq, President Obama and Iraq Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki presented a positive portrait of the evolving relationship between their two countries.

2009-07-24.abstract: A South African court ruled in a landmark decision that when a husband dies without a will in a polygamous marriage, all his wives are guaranteed rights of inheritance.

2009-07-24.abstract: The ousted Honduran president, Manuel Zelaya, left Managua, Nicaragua’s capital, on Thursday with a caravan of supporters in a bid to re-enter Honduras over land.

2009-07-24.abstract: The law would allow Sunday retail openings in about 500 tourist areas and in some large cities.

2009-07-24.title: Afghanistan’s Leader Skips an Election Debate, Claiming Bias
2009-07-24.abstract: President Hamid Karzai said he had not been given enough time to prepare and claimed that the channel organizing the debate was biased.

2009-07-24.abstract: The first fiber-optic cables connecting East Africa to the rest of the world brought hopes of cheaper and faster communications to some of the globe’s least connected.

2009-07-25.title: Lebanon: U.N. Official Points to Hezbollah in Blasts
2009-07-25.abstract: The chief of United Nations peacekeeping forces told the Security Council that explosions in southern Lebanon on July 14 most likely came from an illegal Hezbollah arms cache.

2009-07-25.abstract: Police officers in Moscow broke up a memorial service for the human rights worker Natalya Estemirova on Thursday and detained the organizer of the event.

2009-07-25.abstract: A handful of intrepid aviators are gearing up to re-enact Louis Blériot’s crossing of the English Channel on July 25, the 100th anniversary of the flight.

2009-07-25.abstract: The ousted president, Manuel Zelaya, briefly stepped a few feet inside Honduran territory on Friday afternoon and demanded that he be allowed to return to power.

2009-07-26.title: In Mexico, the Violence Continues
2009-07-26.abstract: Two and a half years after the Mexican government started its war against drug cartels, the crackdown has, in some places, only made life more dangerous.

2009-07-26.title: Hamilton Wins Hungarian Grand Prix
2009-07-26.abstract: It was the 10th victory of Lewis Hamilton’s career, and his second in Budapest, where he won in 2007.

2009-07-26.title: French President Hospitalized
2009-07-26.abstract: President Nicolas Sarkozy was taken to a hospital for tests after getting sick while jogging, the government said. Press reports said his health was not in serious danger.

2009-07-26.title: I.M.F. Approves $2.6 Billion Sri Lanka Loan
2009-07-26.abstract: The International Monetary Fund approved a $2.6 billion loan to help Sri Lanka shore up its economy.

2009-07-27.title: Ultra-Orthodox Settlers in the West Bank
2009-07-27.abstract: A look at the growing population inside the West Bank's Ultra-Orthodox settlements of Modiin Illit and Beitar Illit.

2009-07-27.title: In West Bank Settlements, Sign of Hope for a Deal
2009-07-27.abstract: Two growing ultra-Orthodox settlements offer the surprising potential for Israeli compromise with the Palestinians.

2009-07-27.abstract: After being displaced by the army’s offensives against the Taliban in the Swat Valley, residents of Mingora have returned to military rule.

2009-07-27.title: Files Vanished, Young Chinese Lose the Future
2009-07-27.abstract: Officials are suspected of stealing academic records and selling them to underachievers seeking better prospects.

2009-07-27.title: Terror Ties Run Deep in Pakistan, Mumbai Case Shows
2009-07-27.abstract: Sympathies for Lashkar-e-Taiba and its jihadist and anti-Indian culture are raising a serious challenge to any long-lasting moves to dismantle the network.

2009-07-28.title: Landowners Still in Exile From Unstable Pakistan Area
2009-07-28.abstract: After fleeing the Taliban, landowners are not coming back to the Swat Valley, in a blow to Pakistan’s effort to restore stability.

2009-07-28.title: U.S. Turns Off News Billboard Atop Its Mission in Havana
2009-07-28.abstract: The Obama administration disconnected the billboard outside the American diplomatic mission that was used to tweak the Cuban government with pro-democracy messages.

2009-07-28.title: Huge Profits Spell Doom for a 400-Acre Market
2009-07-28.abstract: A parallel city runs afoul of Prime Minister Vladimir V. Putin’s vision of the new Russia.

2009-07-28.title: A President Kicked Out, but Not Alone in Defiance
2009-07-28.abstract: Hundreds of Hondurans have answered a call from the deposed president, Manuel Zelaya, to join him in Nicaragua.

2009-07-28.title: China: Uighur’s Visit Criticized
2009-07-28.abstract: China criticized Japan on Monday for allowing a visit by Rebiya Kadeer, an exiled Uighur leader who China said incited riots in the Xinjiang region this month.

2009-07-29.title: In Venezuela, Plantations of Cacao Stir Bitterness
2009-07-29.abstract: A luxury crop destined for foreigners has turned into a contentious, and sometimes violent, political issue.

2009-07-29.title: China Seeks Assurances That U.S. Will Cut Its Deficit
2009-07-29.abstract: The rising American deficit is a concern for China because it could put at risk China’s vast holdings of Treasury securities and other dollar-based assets.

2009-07-29.title: Venezuela's Cacao Plantations
2009-07-29.abstract: The passions surrounding the cacao crop in Venezuela have turned it into a contentious, sometimes violent, political issue.

2009-07-29.title: Venezuela's Faded Cacao Crop
2009-07-29.abstract: Venezuelan cacao beans have been coveted by high-end chocolate makers around the world since the 17th century.  But cacao farmers today struggle against a daunting bureaucracy and emboldened squatters.

2009-07-29.title: Car Bomb Wounds Dozens in Spain
2009-07-29.abstract: Spanish authorities blamed the bombing outside a police barracks on the Basque separatist group ETA.

2009-07-30.title: A Polarized Moldova Votes, Mindful of West and Russia
2009-07-30.abstract: Europe’s poorest country is still rattled by riots in April that left three people dead and government buildings burned.

2009-07-30.title: Arrests of Sunni Leaders Rise in Baghdad
2009-07-30.abstract: An increase in Iraqi arrests of former Sunni insurgents in the Awakening movement may reflect a decline in American influence.

2009-07-30.title: Scourge of the Poor
2009-07-30.abstract: Paco, a highly addictive drug, has become the greatest social challenge facing the shantytowns in Buenos Aires.

2009-07-30.title: Lost in an Abyss of Drugs, and Entangled by Poverty
2009-07-30.abstract: Paco, a highly addictive drug, has become the greatest social challenge facing shantytowns in Buenos Aires.

2009-07-30.title: South Korean Fishing Boat Detained by North
2009-07-30.abstract: The detainment of the fishing boat after it accidentally crossed into North Korean waters will likely complicate the tense relations between the two countries.

2009-07-31.title: German Program Cuts Jobless Ranks, for Now
2009-07-31.abstract: The unemployment rate is below that of the United States, but economists are concerned that government-subsidized retraining may mask worrisome trends.

2009-07-31.title: Fishermen Scoop Up Sri Lanka’s Peace Dividend
2009-07-31.abstract: With the end of a long civil war, Sri Lanka’s government has eased restrictions on fishing in the waters off a major harbor.

2009-07-31.title: Regulator Focuses on Airbus Part
2009-07-31.abstract: Europe’s main air safety regulator plans to recommend that all large Airbus jets equipped with the type of air speed sensor that malfunctioned on an Air France plane that crashed last month replace the part as a precaution.

2009-07-31.title: Gayatri Devi, 90, a Maharani and a Lawmaker, Dies
2009-07-31.abstract: The former Maharani of Jaipur was a beauty Vogue once described as “a dream in sari and jewels,” and an advocate for women’s freedom.

2009-07-31.title: Zhuo Lin, the Widow of an Ex-Leader of China, Is Dead at 93
2009-07-31.abstract: Ms. Zhuo was a Communist stalwart, and she helped her hisband Deng Xiaoping survive political purges and become the country’s paramount leader during much of the post-Mao era.
